items.body_excerpt: Former Knicks guard and NBA champion Iman Shumpert stopped by courtside after Game 1 of the NBA Finals to join Danny Green and Harrison Sanford on Inside the Green Room. Shumpert explains why the Knicks showed almost no emotion after taking Game 1 in San Antonio, why New York…

items.title: A fan ran onto the court midway through fourth quarter of Game 1, attempting to take a selfie with Victor Wembanyama.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
A fan ran onto the court midway through fourth quarter of Game 1, attempting to take a selfie with Victor Wembanyama.

"I've never been in that situation," Wembanyama said. "I didn't know how to act. It really surprised me..."

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: Wembanyama 'really surprised' as fan rushes court for selfie during NBA Finals
media.description: A fan disrupted play in Game 1 of the NBA Finals when he ran onto the court and attempted to take a selfie with the 22-year-old Frenchman.

items.title: The Knicks getting good minutes tonight from Shamet, Deuce, Mitch and Alvarado really underscored the value of Mike Brown seeing the regular season as an opportunity to explore what his bench might be able to give, and to show he trusted them to give it. yhoo.it/42WJkQ3
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: The Knicks getting good minutes tonight from Shamet, Deuce, Mitch and Alvarado really underscored the value of Mike Brown seeing the regular season as an opportunity to explore what his bench might be able to give, and to show he trusted them to give it. yhoo.it/42WJkQ3

images.alt: “You know, Steve Kerr, Gregg Popovich — they were guys that went deep into their bench, and they both always used to say, ‘It's not about now, it's about the postseason,’” Brown said after Game 3. “You know, you keep guys engaged by doing that, and you do develop not just the bench, but the team as well, because guys get used to playing with other guys, just in case someone goes down.”

items.title: Long way to go, but: make it nine of 11 playoff series in the Brunson era, and seven straight. yhoo.it/4o2OR0Q
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: Long way to go, but: make it nine of 11 playoff series in the Brunson era, and seven straight. yhoo.it/4o2OR0Q

images.alt:
Prediction: Knicks in 6

To win a series without home-court advantage, you’ve got to get a game on the road. The Knicks have done that in eight of the 10 playoff series they’ve played in the Brunson era, including the last six. They enter this matchup with a significant rest advantage, with all five Spurs starters having played more minutes in these playoffs than any member of the Knicks. They won’t have the best player in the series; they’re also deeper in offensive talent than the Thunder team that just took the Spurs the distance without two of its three best creators.

items.title: More amused than concerned, Wemby did what the intruder wanted. He stood in the background and smiled.
items.author: Mike Finger
items.body_excerpt:
More amused than concerned, Wemby did what the intruder wanted. He stood in the background and smiled.

If he looked unsure of himself, it was understandable.

“I’ve never been in that situation.”

He’ll be better prepared next time. For intruders of all kinds.
 www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: Why another Victor Wembanyama adjustment might make Knicks, intruders wary
media.description: Thus far, in Victor Wembanyama’s experience, the Spurs falling behind in a playoff series never has proven to be a problem.
